mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance or negative mean during periods of declining FEDFUNDS over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly increases in the industrial production index signal rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 returns during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 5-day forward returns during identified post-shock recovery periods, with mean returns below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates and pressures equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in over 60 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC for 60-day forward returns over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in new housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, generating immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: HOUST growth shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 110, supply-side constraints dominate during late-cycle expansions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month due to earnings downgrade fears.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of months where INDPRO is below 110 during late-cycle expansions.
